definitions.pageDescription: FMEA Rating Scales for Severity, Occurrence, and Detection (SOD)
definitions.rpnFormula: RPN = Severity × Occurrence × Detection. Use these tables to assign consistent ratings during FMEA analysis. The final customer should always be considered first. If both effects occur, use the higher of two Severities.
definitions.severityDesc: Severity is the ranking associated with the most serious effect for a given failure mode.
definitions.occurrenceDesc: Occurrence is the likelihood that a specific cause/mechanism will occur, based on failure rates.
definitions.detectionDesc: Detection is a ranking of the ability of current controls to detect the cause/mechanism or failure mode before product reaches the customer.
definitions.criticalityDesc: Define what each criticality level (1-5) means across Safety, Production, Environment, and Reputation dimensions.
definitions.criticalityLogicIntro: Ratings on four dimensions (1–5 each) produce a criticality score, an equipment level, and which maintenance strategy task frequencies apply.
definitions.criticalityLogicStep1: Safety, Production, Environmental, and Reputation. 1 = lowest impact, 5 = highest.
definitions.criticalityLogicStep2Title: Step 2: Criticality score and equipment level
definitions.criticalityLogicScore: Criticality score (0–100) uses the same weighted formula as observations—for reporting and comparison.
definitions.criticalityLogicScoreFormula: Score = round((Safety×25 + Production×20 + Environmental×15 + Reputation×10) / 3.5), maximum 100.
definitions.criticalityLogicLevelIntro: Equipment level is based on the highest dimension ratings, not on the score:
definitions.criticalityLogicLevelSafety: Safety critical — Safety ≥ 4, or any dimension = 5
definitions.criticalityLogicLevelProduction: Production critical — Production ≥ 4, or max dimension ≥ 4 (when not safety critical)
definitions.criticalityLogicStep3Title: Step 3: Maintenance strategy band (High / Medium / Low)
definitions.criticalityLogicStep3: Maintenance task frequencies use three bands. The score does not select the band—the equipment level does:
definitions.criticalityLogicBandHigh: High (more frequent tasks) — Safety critical or Production critical
definitions.criticalityLogicBandLow: Low (less frequent tasks) — equipment level Low
definitions.criticalityLogicNote: The maintenance program shows your equipment criticality and which strategy band was used for task frequencies.

1.label: Minimal
1.safety: No safety impact. Normal operating conditions.

2.label: Low
2.safety: Near miss or minor discomfort. No injury.
2.environment: Negligible environmental impact. Within permit limits.
2.reputation: Minor customer complaint. Internal reporting only.

3.label: Medium
3.safety: Minor injury requiring first aid. Recordable incident.
3.environment: Minor environmental impact. Contained spill or emission.
3.reputation: Local media coverage. Customer dissatisfaction. Internal investigation required.

4.label: High
4.safety: Serious injury requiring hospitalization. Lost time incident.
4.production: Major production loss (>50%). Extended downtime (>24 hours).
4.environment: Significant environmental impact. On-site contamination requiring remediation.
4.reputation: National media coverage. Significant customer complaints. Regulatory scrutiny.

5.label: Critical
5.safety: Fatality or permanent disability. Immediate danger to personnel.
5.production: Complete plant shutdown. Total loss of production capacity (100%).
5.environment: Major environmental disaster. Significant off-site contamination requiring regulatory notification.
5.reputation: International media coverage. Severe damage to company reputation. Loss of operating license possible.

observations.narration: Observations record equipment issues. AssetIQ scores them and drives investigations and actions.
observations.actionHint: Open Observations anytime from the menu.

hierarchy-open.title: Open the equipment hierarchy
hierarchy-open.narration: You can start from the equipment tree. Open the hierarchy panel to browse assets by site, area, and unit.
hierarchy-open.actionHint: Desktop: use the panel icon in the header. Mobile: Menu → Equipment Hierarchy.

hierarchy-select.title: Select equipment
hierarchy-select.narration: Search or expand the tree until you find the affected equipment. Click a row to open its actions.
hierarchy-select.actionHint: Desktop: right-click a row. Mobile: tap the equipment name.

hierarchy-add.title: Add observation from hierarchy
hierarchy-add.narration: Open the equipment menu, then choose Add Observation to start the assistant with that equipment already linked.
hierarchy-add.actionHint: Tap or right-click the equipment row, then choose Add Observation — or tap Next to continue.

report.title: Or use the + button
report.narration: You can also report from anywhere with the + button — useful when you already know what to describe.

describe.title: Describe what you saw
describe.narration: Type the issue in plain language. Mention equipment name or tag if you can — AssetIQ will match equipment and suggest a failure mode.
describe.actionHint: Edit the example text or type your own issue, then continue.
describe.prefillMessage: High vibration on Pump P-101 during operation.

send.title: Submit the report
send.narration: Press Send. AssetIQ summarises your report, matches equipment, and asks you to confirm before creating the observation.
send.actionHint: Click Send when you are ready — or tap Next to continue the tour.

complete.title: You are ready
complete.narration: You can record observations from the hierarchy or the + button. Then validate the failure mode, build an action plan, and track exposure reduction.
complete.actionHint: Finish the tour and try reporting a real observation.

observations.title: Your home screen
observations.narration: Simple mode keeps the essentials on one screen — equipment, observations, and tasks. This is where you start every shift.
observations.actionHint: Tap Next to learn how to report your first observation.

hierarchy-open.title: Open equipment
hierarchy-open.narration: Start from the equipment you are working on. Tap Equipment to browse the plant hierarchy.
hierarchy-open.actionHint: Tap the Equipment button — or tap Next to continue the tour.

hierarchy-select.title: Find your equipment
hierarchy-select.narration: Search or expand the tree until you find the affected equipment. Tap a row to see its actions.
hierarchy-select.actionHint: Tap an equipment name to open its menu.

hierarchy-add.title: Add observation from equipment
hierarchy-add.narration: Tap the equipment row to open its menu, then choose Add Observation to start the assistant with that equipment already linked.
hierarchy-add.actionHint: Tap the equipment row, then choose Add Observation — or tap Next to continue.

report.title: Or use the + button
report.narration: The blue + button is always available — use it when you already know what to describe.

describe.title: Describe what you saw
describe.narration: Type the issue in plain language. Mention equipment name or tag if you can — AssetIQ will match equipment and suggest a failure mode.
describe.actionHint: Edit the example text or type your own issue, then continue.
describe.prefillMessage: High vibration on Pump P-101 during operation.

send.title: Submit the report
send.narration: Press Send. AssetIQ summarises your report, matches equipment, and asks you to confirm before creating the observation.
send.actionHint: Tap Send when you are ready — or tap Next to continue the tour.

complete.title: You are ready
complete.narration: You can report from Equipment or the + button. Open Observations on the home screen to review what you logged.
complete.actionHint: Finish the tour and try reporting a real observation.

progressObservationTour.keyboardHint: Use ← → to navigate · Space to play / pause · Esc to exit
progressObservationTour.mobileHint: Use the highlighted control, then tap Next

open-observation.title: Open an Observation
open-observation.narration: Select an observation to review its details, analyze the issue, and determine the next actions.

review-failure-mode.title: Review Failure Mode
review-failure-mode.narration: Verify that the suggested failure mode matches the observed problem. This failure mode drives risk scoring, recommendations, and future analysis.

link-failure-mode.title: Connect Reliability Knowledge
link-failure-mode.narration: Open the ⋯ menu in the top-right corner and choose Link Failure Mode. Connect an existing mode from the library, or create a new one with severity, occurrence, and detection.

link-equipment.title: Connect Equipment Context
link-equipment.narration: Use the same ⋯ menu to link the affected equipment from the hierarchy. This adds criticality, asset context, and historical observations.

ai-insights.title: Review AI Analysis
ai-insights.narration: Review AI-generated insights, likely causes, and risk indicators to better understand the issue.

recommended-actions.title: Evaluate Recommendations
recommended-actions.narration: Review actions suggested by the failure mode library and AI analysis. Determine which actions should be implemented.

generate-recommendations.title: Need More Actions?
generate-recommendations.narration: Use the sparkles button in the Recommended Actions header to generate additional mitigation and prevention actions with AI.

build-action-plan.title: Create Action Plan
build-action-plan.narration: Promote recommended actions into the Action Plan. These actions become tracked work items used to resolve the issue.

refine-actions.title: Finalize Action Plan
refine-actions.narration: Edit actions, adjust priorities, assign disciplines, and add any missing tasks required to fully address the issue.

validate-plan.title: Validate Actions
validate-plan.narration: Validate actions before execution to ensure the proposed plan is technically sound and approved.

start-investigation.title: Need Root Cause Analysis?
start-investigation.narration: For complex or recurring issues, launch a full investigation. AssetIQ can perform AI-assisted causal analysis and create an investigation linked to this observation.

complete.title: Observation Ready for Execution
complete.narration: Great work. The observation is now linked to reliability knowledge, connected to equipment, supported by recommendations, and backed by an actionable resolution plan.
complete.actionHint: Finish the tour and apply these steps to a real observation.
